LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PFIZER INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. AT THE SAME TIME, IT IS APPARENT THAT PLAINTIFFS' LACK EVIDENCE TO PROVE EXPOSURE AND THAT DEFENDANT HAS RAISED SERIOUS QUESTIONS AS TO PLAINTIFFS' GOOD FAITH COMPLIANCE WITH THE DISCOVERY MANDATES OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE WITH RESPECT TO THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS AND DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION NOTICES. AT THE SAME TIME, DEFENDANT WAS SERVED ON DECEMBER 31, 2007 BUT DID NOT SERVE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES UNTIL JULY 23, 2008. DEFENDANTS ALSO HAVE NOT SOUGHT A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE BASED ON PLAINTIFFS' DISCOVERY CONDUCT. THE COURT ADMITS THE TESTIMONY OF GENCO AND METTRIC BASED ON THE REPLY EVIDENCE BUT THAT TESTIMONY IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES:

Michael J. Boland, Esq., for defendant and moving party: The C.P. Hall Company
Robert Shickland, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Consuelo Lopez

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted with modification. Defendant C.P. Hall's motion to compel verifications to standard, form and special interrogatories and requests for admissions is granted. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied.
MODIFICATION. Compliance by: Noon December 29, 2008.
Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

MOTION To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories To Plaintiffs, Set One, And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Stephen M. Fishback, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Consuelo Lopez
Steven H. Frankel, Esq., and Edward K. Low, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Rapid American Corporation

After hearing, Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to discovery is Granted in part. Defendant's motion for a protective order granted in part. The objections, other that based on privilege are stricken. Defendant shall produce any further responsive documents if any, by January 16, 2009 or submit a verification by declaration that all such documents have been produced after a reasonable and diligent search. If any documents are withheld pursuant to privilege, a privilege log shall be provided by January 16, 2009. The Court further orders that defendant is precluded from introducing into evidence any documents that have not been provided to plaintiff by January 16, 2009. Lastly, the deposition of the person most knowledgeable shall be deemed to have been taken in other specified cases, with the transcript to be have been taken in other specified cases, with the transcript to be admissible in those actions as well. Plaintiff not notice a further deposition of the PMK without first attempting to meet and confer. Plaintiff may apply to the court for leave to conduct any further deposition.
Court Reporter: Mary Jackson, CSR # 8688

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PFIZER INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES.
APPEARANCES: J. Bruce Jackson, Esq. of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP 775-1206 for pltfs & opposing parties; Natalie Nuttall, Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP 267-4054 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. AT THE SAME TIME, IT IS APPARENT THAT PLAINTIFFS' LACK EVIDENCE TO PROVE EXPOSURE AND THAT DEFENDANT HAS RAISED SERIOUS QUESTIONS AS TO PLAINTIFFS' GOOD FAITH COMPLIANCE WITH THE DISCOVERY MANDATES OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE WITH RESPECT TO THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS AND DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION NOTICES. AT THE SAME TIME, DEFENDANT WAS SERVED ON DECEMBER 31, 2007 BUT DID NOT SERVE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES UNTIL JULY 23, 2008. DEFENDANTS ALSO HAVE NOT SOUGHT A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE BASED ON PLAINTIFFS' DISCOVERY CONDUCT. THE COURT ADMITS THE TESTIMONY OF GENCO AND METTRIC BASED ON THE REPLY EVIDENCE BUT THAT TESTIMONY IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. Plaintiff to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION For An Order Compelling Deft Rt Vanderbilt Co. To Respond To Discovery Requests, Set Two; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Bruce Jackson, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Consuelo Lopez and Mark Lopez
Mimi M. Lee, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: RT Vanderbilt Company
Court Reporter: Renee Bush, CSR#5793

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Consuelo Lopez's motion to compel further responses to discovery (set #2) as to defendant RT Vanderbilt is denied. While the requests were served while discovery was open, the responses were due after the close of discovery; accordingly, the objection pursuant to CCP Section 2024.020 was proper. In addition, plaintiff failed to file a statement of items in dispute pursuant to California Rule of Court 3.1020. The Court finds that the motion was made without substantial justification. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $2,000 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-25-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT J.T. THORPE & SON, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Bruce Jackson, Esq. of Keller, Fishback & Jackson LLP 775-1206 for pltfs & opposing parties; Megan W. Wendell, Esq. of Bassi, Martini, Edlin & Blum LLP 397-9006 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN ON CAUSATION BY RELYING ON PLAINTIFFS ORIGINAL ANSWERS TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES CANNOT BE RELIED UPON BECAUSE: 1)THEY ARE UNVERIFIED; 2)THEY ARE HEARSAY AND 3)THE RESPONDING PARTY MAY NOT RELY ON ITS OWN RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES. CCP SECTION 2030.410.
MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED.
(a) DEFENDANT IS NOT LIABLE FOR STRICT LIABILITY IN TORT AS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT MANUFACTURED OR "SUPPLIED" AN ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCT.
(b)THERE IS NO EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE FALSE REPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION.
(c)THERE IS NO EVIDENCE OF MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO SUPPORT THE PUNITIVE DAMAGE CLAIM.
(d)THE LOSS OF CONSORTIUM CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE IT IS DERIVATIVE.
Order signed in open court. (302/JKS/PB) JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR #4761
